content.question: To what extent does the measured 10% value-added effect for students in 6/8-year gimnaziums reflect genuine pedagogical advantage of the 6+6 structure (longer developmental runway) versus peer effects, teacher sorting, or unmeasured selection differences present at the admission threshold itself?
content.reduction_path: Design a quasi-experimental study with pre-admission test-score and sociodemographic data matched across 4-year and 6/8-year track entrants, tracking students forward through secondary schooling; use regression discontinuity or instrumental-variable methods to isolate within-student gains attributable to school structure vs. compositional effects.
